hypothesis: E-47: Soft bbox extent targets for bbox-only samples (MoCA CSV). Hard rectangles are replaced with 2D Gaussian falloff from bbox center (sigma proportional to bbox dimensions, sigma_factor=0.3, clamped to [1,5] pixels at 28x28). Real PNG masks stay hard binary. Tests whether softer spatial structure from bbox-derived targets helps — Gaussian encodes 'center is definitely foreground, boundaries are uncertain', which may guide the model to learn better extent. Core prediction: softer bbox targets reduce the 'hard edge' artifact and improve large-object IoU. If pf_mIoU > E-45 OR area_ratio closer to 1.0, direction is valid.
head: dense_fg_aux
